Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto Honkyō shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:pages needing proper jawiki and history import]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: Importing a single revision of my older page. Because the history was messed up. Everything else here was just mine but the jawiki stuff will be imported soon
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakagyo-ku|ja|中京区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagyo-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=}}, a stone traditionally used during the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} to offer {{ill|Shinsen|ja|神饌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen|qq=}}. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ojin|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of {{ill|Gozu Tenno|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tenno|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}} and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}}. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto Honkyō shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:pages needing proper jawiki and history import]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakagyo-ku|ja|中京区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagyo-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=}}, a stone traditionally used during the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} to offer {{ill|Shinsen|ja|神饌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen|qq=}}. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ojin|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of {{ill|Gozu Tenno|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tenno|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}} and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}}. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto Honkyō shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:pages needing proper jawiki and history import]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]

A divine spirit of {{ill|Gozu Tenno|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tenno|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}} and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto Honkyō shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]

Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ojin|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of {{ill|Gozu Tenno|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tenno|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}} and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}}. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto Honkyō shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] {{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=}}, a stone traditionally used during the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} to offer {{ill|Shinsen|ja|神饌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen|qq=}}. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ojin|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of {{ill|Gozu Tenno|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tenno|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}} and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}}. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto Honkyō shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto Honkyō shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakagyo-ku|ja|中京区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagyo-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=}}, a stone traditionally used during the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} to offer {{ill|Shinsen|ja|神饌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen|qq=}}. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ojin|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of {{ill|Gozu Tenno|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tenno|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}} and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}}. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto Honkyō shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shrine | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | consecration_year = {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakagyo-ku|ja|中京区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakagyo-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=}}, a stone traditionally used during the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}} to offer {{ill|Shinsen|ja|神饌|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen|qq=}}. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=}} * {{ill|Emperor Ojin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ojin|qq=}} == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in {{ill|Jogan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jogan|qq=}} 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of {{ill|Gozu Tenno|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tenno|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Harima Province|ja|播磨国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima Province|qq=}} and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the {{ill|Gion Matsuri|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gion Matsuri|qq=}}. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|経津主神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futsunushi|qq=}} === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niinamesai|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** {{ill|Hankyu Kyoto Line|ja|阪急京都本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hankyu Kyoto Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|ja|大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: {{ill|Shijō-Ōmiya Station|ja|四条大宮駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shijō-Ōmiya Station|qq=}} (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}} | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:中京区の建築物
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:中京区の建築物]]

Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:中京区の歴史]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]]
