Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:refresh]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:神社本教の神社]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

{{Shrine | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

Nagi Shrine (left) and Hayabusa Shrine (right) | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|0|12.11|N|135|44|38.69|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Shrine | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

[[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

revision.comment: Bot: sync infobox from Wikidata (P18,P131,P625,P112,P825,P361,P140,P149)
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11538723]] Wikidata name: Nagi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.003364|N|135.744081|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = | prefecture = {{ill|Nakagyō Ward|qid=Q1075455}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ|1=Nageia nagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3274110}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

[[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:user:immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "user:immanuelle" to "User:Immanuelle"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11538723]] Wikidata name: Nagi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.003364|N|135.744081|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Nakagyō Ward|qid=Q1075455}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=|1=Mibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1926654}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ|1=Nageia nagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3274110}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:User:Immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11538723]] Wikidata name: Nagi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.003364|N|135.744081|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Nakagyō Ward|qid=Q1075455}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=|1=Mibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1926654}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ|1=Nageia nagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3274110}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:User:Immanuelle/Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[sn:User:Immanuelle/隼神社]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Nagi Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}}

[[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11538723}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11538723]] Wikidata name: Nagi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Nagi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nagi-jinja shaden.JPG | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Nakagyo-ku, Mibu Naginomiya-cho 18-2 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.003364|N|135.744081|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined Deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] | consecration_year = [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = Former Gion Shrine | festival = 3rd Sunday of May | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Nakagyō Ward|qid=Q1075455}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Nagi Shrine''' (梛神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Mibu|ja|壬生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mibu|qq=|1=Mibu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1926654}}, [[:en:Nakagyō-ku, Kyoto|Nakagyo-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Commonly referred to as "Former Gion Shrine," the site also houses the {{ill|Hayabusa Shrine|ja|隼神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayabusa Shrine|qq=}}, an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. == Overview == The shrine's sacred site contains the {{ill|Gokuseki (offering stone)|ja|御供石|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokuseki (offering stone)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a stone traditionally used during the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]] to offer [[:en:Shinsen|Shinsen]]. Originally located in Shimogyo Ward, it was moved to the shrine grounds in 1932.{{Cite book|author=Toshinori Takemura |title=Showa Kyoto Meisho Zue |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |year=1984/11/5 |page=317}} [[ja:梛神社]] == Enshrined Deities == The enshrined deities include the following:Shrine explanation panel. ; Main deity * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] ; Additional Deities * {{ill|Uka-no-Mitama|ja|宇賀御魂命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uka-no-Mitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ojin]] == History == According to legend, the shrine was established in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 11 (869) during an outbreak of disease in the capital. A divine spirit of [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]] (another form of Susanoo-no-Mikoto) was brought from {{ill|Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hiromine Shrine|lt=Hirohata Shrine|ja|広峰神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirohata Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 広峯神社}} in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]] and enshrined within a grove of {{ill|Nagi|ja|梛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ナギ|1=Nageia nagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3274110}} trees.{{Cite book|author=Takakatsu Ishida |title=Kyoto Historical Site Dictionary: Compact Edition |publisher=Shin Jinbutsu Oraisha |year=2001/10/10 |page=200-201 |isbn=4404029381}} When the divine spirit was later transferred to Yasaka, the local villagers carried decorated umbrellas and spears while performing music and dances to escort the deity. This procession is said to be the origin of the umbrella floats in the [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Matsuri]]. Because of this connection, the shrine is known as the "Former Gion Shrine." The shrine remained a small sanctuary until its restoration during the Meiji (1874) and Showa (1929) periods, resulting in its current form. == Hayabusa Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hayabusa Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagi-jinja Hayabusa-jinja.JPG|250px]]
Hayabusa Shrine | location = | coordinates = | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Main Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate name = | festival = November 17 | Rituals = }} '''Hayabusa Shrine''' (隼神社) is located within the Nagi Shrine grounds and is an ancient {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}. [[File:隼神社.jpg|thumb|200px|right|Hayabusa Shrine stone pillar]] === Enshrined Deities === ; Main deity * {{ill|Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|ja|建甕槌神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Additional deity * [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] === History === The exact origins of the shrine are unknown, but it is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] (860–874) as "After-In Hayabusa Shrine," ascending to the Rank of {{ill|Jūshi-i Jō|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jūshi-i Jō|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}}.{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} It is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] (927), where it received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niinamesai]].{{Harvid|隼社（平凡社）|1979th year }} Originally located in the Suzaku-in area, it was later moved to its current location in the Nagi Shrine grounds in 1918.The shrine explanation panel states 1918, while other sources mention 1920. == Festivals == Annual rituals include the following: * Monthly Festivals: 1st, 15th, 17th of each month * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 2–3: Setsubun Festival * February 17: Kinen Festival * 3rd Sunday of May: Parish Festival * June 30: Summer Purification, Chinowa Kuguri * 3rd Sunday of November: Hayabusa Shrine Festival, Fire Offering Festival * November 23: Labor Thanksgiving Festival * December 31: Grand Purification Ceremony == Access == ; Address * Nagi Shrine: Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture ; Transportation * Train ** [[:en:Hankyu Kyoto Main Line|Hankyu Kyoto Line]]: [[:en:Ōmiya Station|Ōmiya Station (Kyoto)]] (4-minute walk west) ** {{ill|Keifuku Arashiyama Line|ja|京福嵐山本線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keifuku Arashiyama Line|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [[:en:Shijō-Ōmiya Station|Shijō-Ōmiya Station]] (4-minute walk west) * Bus ** Kyoto City Bus: Disembark at "Mibu Temple Road" == References == {{Reflist}} {{Reflist|group="note"}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Nagi-jinja}} * [https://motogion-nagijinja.or.jp/ Official website of Nagi Shrine] * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=1901 Hayabusa Shrine - Kokugakuin University Shinto and Shrine Database] {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|隼神社|version=66993662|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:隼神社]] [[ja:%E6%A2%9B%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE#%E9%9A%BC%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{translated page|ja|梛神社|version=102960797|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Nakagyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines of Jinja Honkyō]] {{Commons category|Nagi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11538723}}
